description:
## Background

After the first successful selection, Beadsmith must behave like a desktop client rather than a one-directory launcher. Users need their successfully used Workspaces remembered, ordered predictably, restored safely after restart, and removable without touching repository data. A saved path can also disappear temporarily; silently deleting that user choice would make recovery harder.

## What to build

Complete the Workspace Catalog lifecycle in the compact inline sidebar selector. Restore the most recently successful Current Workspace provisionally at startup, show remembered entries in MRU order with basename and full path, keep missing entries as Unavailable Workspaces, and allow local-only removal.

## Technical Approach

- Persist at most 100 Workspace roots. Re-adding an exact known path must retain one entry. Promote an entry to MRU only after it validates and fully loads as Current; picker open, selection, validation failure, and load failure do not affect order.
- Startup restoration must use the normal validation-and-load transaction. A remembered path becomes Current only after full load and store success. A missing path remains Unavailable; a valid path whose load fails remains a retryable known Workspace; neither is Current.
- Render remembered entries adjacent to the Current Workspace control. Distinguish unavailable entries accessibly, retain full-path disambiguation, and provide an explicit remove action.
- Removing the Current Workspace clears it and shows the empty chooser. Never silently select the next MRU entry. Removal needs no confirmation because it affects only Beadsmith local catalog state.

## Considerations

- Custom Workspace naming is out of scope; basename plus full path is the product identity display.
- Special symlink handling is out of scope. Preserve ordinary validated root paths rather than adding alias-deduplication rules.
- A catalog entry is not proof its repository remains usable; validation and load still gate becoming Current.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The last successfully Current Workspace is restored only after normal validation, complete Issue Explorer load, and persistence success.
- [ ] The selector displays known Workspaces in success-only MRU order, capped at 100 entries, with basename and full path.
- [ ] Missing or unreadable paths remain visible as retryable Unavailable Workspaces until explicitly removed.
- [ ] Removing a non-current entry affects only the local catalog; removing Current clears the app to the empty chooser without auto-switching.
- [ ] Failed validation or loading never promotes a Workspace in MRU order or presents it as Current.

## Test Plan

- Rust tests for cap eviction, exact-path re-add, success-only promotion, unavailable retention, restore outcomes, and removal rules.
- Frontend integration tests for MRU presentation, unavailable state, path disambiguation, removal, and empty chooser after Current removal.
- Persistence/restart integration test using the backend-owned store and normal switch transaction.
- Run `cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, and TypeScript checking.

## Storage-health amendment

Catalog restoration must distinguish an Unavailable Workspace from unavailable Workspace memory. A missing or unreadable repository path remains an Unavailable Workspace entry. Malformed, unreadable, or unsupported catalog storage is a separate storage-health state: preserve the source data, offer retry, and allow an explicit reset of Beadsmith local Workspace memory. Never silently recreate an empty catalog over unreadable user data.

The directory picker should default to the Current Workspace root when present, otherwise the most-recent available Workspace root, otherwise the operating-system default. This improves repeat selection without changing catalog identity or MRU order.

### Additional acceptance criteria

- [ ] Storage-health recovery is distinguishable from an Unavailable Workspace and never deletes catalog data without explicit user action.
- [ ] Picker default location follows Current Workspace, then most-recent available Workspace, then the OS default.

### Additional test plan

- Test corrupt catalog recovery and explicit reset independently from missing-repository behavior.
- Test picker default-path selection without treating picker open or cancellation as an MRU event.

comments.text:
## Catalog rule of thumb

The catalog remembers successful user choices; it does not assert that every saved repository remains available. Availability is re-established only by validation and a complete load, which is why unavailable entries stay retryable instead of disappearing.

Changelog: catalog behavior must use the transactional state service, including its typed storage-health state. The selector should retain user data on a corrupted catalog and expose explicit recovery rather than silently resetting Workspace memory.
Changelog: expanded the task body with explicit storage-health recovery and picker default-location behavior so unavailable repositories and unreadable catalog memory cannot be conflated.

title: Restore and manage the Workspace Catalog
